question.text: In a bull call spread, the maximum loss equals:

answer.text: The net premium paid (lower strike call premium minus higher strike call premium received)
The difference between the two strike prices

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Bull call spread max loss = Net premium = Premium paid for lower call - Premium received for higher call. This occurs when USDINR stays at or below the lower strike price. **The Trap:** Max loss is NOT the full premium of the lower call — it's reduced by the premium received from selling the higher call. **Cross-Exam Context:** Spread P&L limits are tested in Series I and VIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** This reduced cost (vs naked long call) is the main advantage of the bull call spread.
